sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Arsenal are struggling with no wins in their last 5 matches, conceding in each game, while Newcastle have shown resilience with no losses in their last 3 matches, including strong victories. Newcastle's recent form indicates a more confident and attacking side compared to Arsenal's inconsistency.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Newcastle have dominated recent encounters, winning both of the last two head-to-head matches against Arsenal with clean sheets (2-0 each time). This suggests a tactical or psychological edge for Newcastle in direct matchups.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Arsenal boast a strong defensive record (fewest goals conceded in the league) and high possession stats, but their recent inability to convert chances (high big chances missed) is a concern. Newcastle's attacking output (68 goals scored) and ability to score early in games could exploit Arsenal's current vulnerabilities.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Arsenal are missing key players like Declan Rice and Gabriel Jesus, potentially weakening their midfield and attack. Newcastle's missing players, such as Joelinton, are impactful but their recent results show depth in squad performance.
